revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''鷺山古墳'''(さぎやまこふん)とは、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]](旧[[児玉町]])下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末...'
revision.text:
'''鷺山古墳'''(さぎやまこふん)とは、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]](旧[[児玉町]])下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]初頭）の[[前方後方墳]]である。東西方向におよそ150ｍ、南北方向に500ｍほどの周囲の水田面から比高差7mで「く」の字形の小高い丘の頂点に築かれており、後方部の墳丘の頂点に標高84.42mの[[三角点]]がある。
鷺山古墳の全長は約60m、後方部の墳丘の高さは5.4ｍ、後方部の一辺が37ｍ、くびれ部の幅11ｍで前方部につながる。後方部は、厳密に方形ではなく、隅の部分が切り落とされたプランを呈している。前方部は初期古墳独特の墳丘が低いばち型（扇状にひらく形）で、10.5ｍまで後方部に対して74度でひらき、35度に屈曲しながら13ｍほど外側にひらく。前方部前面の最大幅は、30ｍである。前方部の盛土は後方部に近い部分が高くされ、標高80.3ｍであった。前方部全面の扇形に広がる部分は一段低くなっている。周溝がほぼ全周すると考えられ、幅は4.3ｍ、深さ1ｍほどである。
前方部と後方部をつなぐくびれ部の西側の周溝から焼成前底部穿孔の二重口縁壺が出土した。[[口縁部]]直径25cm、口縁部下端18cm、胴部の最大径が28.5cm、器高34cmで焼成後に赤彩されている。口縁部の「縁帯」の幅は8cmをはかり、直径1.8cmほどの円形の透かし孔が6対計12個あけられている。底部は6.4cmで焼成前に予めあけられた孔となっている。 そのほか、完形品の碗型土器、二重口縁壺の口縁部破片や、東海地方独特のＳ字状台付甕の胴部と脚部をつなぐ部分の出土など初期古墳の特色を示す遺物が出土している。二重口縁の壺型土器と碗型土器は葬送儀礼の際に用いられ、墳丘に置かれたものが転落して周溝から発見されたと考えられる。
==参考文献==
「鷺山古墳」『埼玉県古式古墳調査報告書』所収,埼玉県史編纂室,昭和61年(1986) {{DEFAULTSORT:こんけんやまこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:都道府県指定史跡]]

'''鷺山古墳'''(さぎやまこふん)とは、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]](旧[[児玉町]])下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]初頭）の[[前方後方墳]]である。東西方向におよそ150ｍ、南北方向に500ｍほどの周囲の水田面から比高差7mで「く」の字形の小高い丘の頂点に築かれており、後方部の墳丘の頂点に標高84.42mの[[三角点]]がある。
鷺山古墳の全長は約60m、後方部の墳丘の高さは5.4ｍ、後方部の一辺が37ｍ、くびれ部の幅11ｍで前方部につながる。後方部は、厳密に方形ではなく、隅の部分が切り落とされたプランを呈している。前方部は初期古墳独特の墳丘が低いばち型（扇状にひらく形）で、10.5ｍまで後方部に対して74度でひらき、35度に屈曲しながら13ｍほど外側にひらく。前方部前面の最大幅は、30ｍである。前方部の盛土は後方部に近い部分が高くされ、標高80.3ｍであった。前方部全面の扇形に広がる部分は一段低くなっている。周溝がほぼ全周すると考えられ、幅は4.3ｍ、深さ1ｍほどである。
前方部と後方部をつなぐくびれ部の西側の周溝から焼成前底部穿孔の二重口縁壺が出土した。[[口縁部]]直径25cm、口縁部下端18cm、胴部の最大径が28.5cm、器高34cmで焼成後に赤彩されている。口縁部の「縁帯」の幅は8cmをはかり、直径1.8cmほどの円形の透かし孔が6対計12個あけられている。底部は6.4cmで焼成前に予めあけられた孔となっている。 そのほか、完形品の碗型土器、二重口縁壺の口縁部破片や、東海地方独特のＳ字状台付甕の胴部と脚部をつなぐ部分の出土など初期古墳の特色を示す遺物が出土している。二重口縁の壺型土器と碗型土器は葬送儀礼の際に用いられ、墳丘に置かれたものが転落して周溝から発見されたと考えられる。
==参考文献==

[[File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鷺山古墳の標柱]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳遠景]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳後方部近景]] [[File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|thumb|300px|right|鷺山古墳後方部墳頂部にある三角点]] '''鷺山古墳'''(さぎやまこふん)とは、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]](旧[[児玉町]])下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]初頭）小沢は[[廻間遺跡|廻間]]Ⅱ式の終末、3世紀後半の終わりごろとし（小沢1995,p.479）、増田は、廻間Ⅲ式の第一段階と位置付ける（増田1997,p.12,p.14）。の[[前方後方墳]]である。東西方向におよそ150ｍ、南北方向に500ｍほどの周囲の水田面から比高差7mで「く」の字形の小高い丘の頂点に築かれており、後方部の墳丘の頂点に標高84.42mの[[三角点]]がある。 鷺山古墳の全長は約60m、後方部の墳丘の高さは5.4ｍ、後方部の一辺が37ｍ、くびれ部の幅11ｍで前方部につながる。後方部は、厳密に方形ではなく、隅の部分が切り落とされたプランを呈している。前方部は初期古墳独特の墳丘が低いばち型（扇状にひらく形）で、10.5ｍまで後方部に対して74度でひらき、35度に屈曲しながら13ｍほど外側にひらく。前方部前面の最大幅は、30ｍである。前方部の盛土は後方部に近い部分が高くされ、標高80.3ｍであった。前方部全面の扇形に広がる部分は一段低くなっている。周溝がほぼ全周すると考えられ、幅は4.3ｍ、深さ1ｍほどである。 前方部と後方部をつなぐくびれ部の西側の周溝から焼成前[[底部穿孔土器|底部穿孔]]の二重口縁壺が出土した。[[口縁部]]直径25cm、口縁部下端18cm、胴部の最大径が28.5cm、器高34cmで焼成後に赤彩されている。口縁部の「縁帯」の幅は8cmをはかり、直径1.8cmほどの円形の透かし孔が6対計12個あけられている。底部は6.4cmで焼成前に予めあけられた孔となっている。 そのほか、完形品の碗型土器、二重口縁壺の口縁部破片や、東海地方独特の[[S字状口縁台付甕]]の胴部と脚部をつなぐ部分の出土など初期古墳の特色を示す遺物が出土している。二重口縁の壺型土器と碗型土器は葬送儀礼の際に用いられ、墳丘に置かれたものが転落して周溝から発見されたと考えられる。 ==脚注==

[[File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鷺山古墳の標柱]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳遠景]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳後方部近景]] [[File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳後方部墳頂部にある三角点]] '''鷺山古墳'''(さぎやまこふん)とは、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]](旧[[児玉町]])下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末～[[4世紀]]初頭）小沢は[[廻間遺跡|廻間]]Ⅱ式の終末、3世紀後半の終わりごろとし（小沢1995,p.479）、増田は、廻間Ⅲ式の第一段階と位置付ける（増田1997,p.12,p.14）。の[[前方後方墳]]である。 == 概要 == 鷺山古墳は東西方向におよそ150m、南北方向に500mほどの周囲の水田面から比高差7mで「く」の字形の小高い丘の頂点に築かれており、後方部の墳丘の頂点に標高84.42mの[[三角点]]がある。 鷺山古墳の全長は約60m、後方部の墳丘の高さは5.4m、後方部の一辺が37m、くびれ部の幅11mで前方部につながる。後方部は、厳密に方形ではなく、隅の部分が切り落とされたプランを呈している。前方部は初期古墳独特の墳丘が低いばち型（扇状にひらく形）で、10.5mまで後方部に対して74度でひらき、35度に屈曲しながら13mほど外側にひらく。前方部前面の最大幅は、30mである。前方部の盛土は後方部に近い部分が高くされ、標高80.3mであった。前方部全面の扇形に広がる部分は一段低くなっている。周溝がほぼ全周すると考えられ、幅は4.3m、深さ1mほどである。 前方部と後方部をつなぐくびれ部の西側の周溝から焼成前[[底部穿孔土器|底部穿孔]]の二重口縁壺が出土した。[[口縁部]]直径25cm、口縁部下端18cm、胴部の最大径が28.5cm、器高34cmで焼成後に赤彩されている。口縁部の「縁帯」の幅は8cmをはかり、直径1.8cmほどの円形の透かし孔が6対計12個あけられている。底部は6.4cmで焼成前に予めあけられた孔となっている。 そのほか、完形品の碗型土器、二重口縁壺の口縁部破片や、東海地方独特の[[S字状口縁台付甕]]の胴部と脚部をつなぐ部分の出土など初期古墳の特色を示す遺物が出土している。二重口縁の壺型土器と碗型土器は葬送儀礼の際に用いられ、墳丘に置かれたものが転落して周溝から発見されたと考えられる。 == 脚注 ==

== 参考文献 == *「鷺山古墳」『埼玉県古式古墳調査報告書』所収、埼玉県史編纂室、1986年（昭和61年） *小沢洋「南関東地方の前方後方墳」『前方後方墳を考える』第一分冊、海考古学フォーラム、1995年（平成7年） *増田逸郎「古墳出現期の北武蔵-前方後方墳成立の要因-」『調査研究報告第10号』[[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|埼玉県立さきたま資料館]]、1997年（平成9年） == 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さきやまこふん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Saigen Jiro がページ「[[鷺山古墳]]」を「[[鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]]」に移動しました: 鳥取県鳥取市に同名古墳が存在するため。

{{otheruses|埼玉県本庄市児玉町にある古墳|鳥取県鳥取市にある古墳|鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)}} [[File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鷺山古墳の標柱]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳遠景]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳後方部近景]] [[File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|鷺山古墳後方部墳頂部にある三角点]] '''鷺山古墳'''(さぎやまこふん)とは、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]][[児玉町]]下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末〜[[4世紀]]初頭）小沢は[[廻間遺跡|廻間]]II式の終末、3世紀後半の終わりごろとし（小沢1995,p.479）、増田は、廻間III式の第一段階と位置付ける（増田1997,p.12,p.14）。の[[前方後方墳]]である。 == 概要 == 鷺山古墳は東西方向におよそ150m、南北方向に500mほどの周囲の水田面から比高差7mで「く」の字形の小高い丘の頂点に築かれており、後方部の墳丘の頂点に標高84.42mの[[三角点]]がある。 鷺山古墳の全長は約60m、後方部の墳丘の高さは5.4m、後方部の一辺が37m、くびれ部の幅11mで前方部につながる。後方部は、厳密に方形ではなく、隅の部分が切り落とされたプランを呈している。前方部は初期古墳独特の墳丘が低いばち型（扇状にひらく形）で、10.5mまで後方部に対して74度でひらき、35度に屈曲しながら13mほど外側にひらく。前方部前面の最大幅は、30mである。前方部の盛土は後方部に近い部分が高くされ、標高80.3mであった。前方部全面の扇形に広がる部分は一段低くなっている。周溝がほぼ全周すると考えられ、幅は4.3m、深さ1mほどである。 前方部と後方部をつなぐくびれ部の西側の周溝から焼成前[[底部穿孔土器|底部穿孔]]の二重口縁壺が出土した。[[口縁部]]直径25cm、口縁部下端18cm、胴部の最大径が28.5cm、器高34cmで焼成後に赤彩されている。口縁部の「縁帯」の幅は8cmをはかり、直径1.8cmほどの円形の透かし孔が6対計12個あけられている。底部は6.4cmで焼成前に予めあけられた孔となっている。 そのほか、完形品の碗型土器、二重口縁壺の口縁部破片や、東海地方独特の[[S字状口縁台付甕]]の胴部と脚部をつなぐ部分の出土など初期古墳の特色を示す遺物が出土している。二重口縁の壺型土器と碗型土器は葬送儀礼の際に用いられ、墳丘に置かれたものが転落して周溝から発見されたと考えられる。 == 脚注 ==
== 参考文献 == *「鷺山古墳」『埼玉県古式古墳調査報告書』所収、埼玉県史編纂室、1986年（昭和61年） *小沢洋「南関東地方の前方後方墳」『前方後方墳を考える』第一分冊、海考古学フォーラム、1995年（平成7年） *増田逸郎「古墳出現期の北武蔵-前方後方墳成立の要因-」『調査研究報告第10号』[[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|埼玉県立さきたま資料館]]、1997年（平成9年） == 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さきやまこふん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

== 参考文献 == *「鷺山古墳」『埼玉県古式古墳調査報告書』所収、埼玉県史編纂室、1986年（昭和61年） *小沢洋「南関東地方の前方後方墳」『前方後方墳を考える』第一分冊、海考古学フォーラム、1995年（平成7年） *増田逸郎「古墳出現期の北武蔵-前方後方墳成立の要因-」『調査研究報告第10号』[[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|埼玉県立さきたま資料館]]、1997年（平成9年） == 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さきやまこふん ほんしようし}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=鷺山古墳 |別名= |所属= |画像=[[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション = 鷺山古墳後方部近景 |所在地=埼玉県本庄市児玉町下浅見字鷺山 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模= |築造年代= |出土品= |史跡指定= |埋葬施設= |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=12|lat_sec=30.4|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=43.7|label=鷺山古墳|mark=前方後方墳-南西.png|marksize=23|caption=埼玉県内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E}}}} }} '''鷺山古墳'''（さぎやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[本庄市]][[児玉町]]下浅見字鷺山にある[[古墳時代]]初頭（[[3世紀]]末〜[[4世紀]]初頭）小沢は[[廻間遺跡|廻間]]II式の終末、3世紀後半の終わりごろとし（小沢1995,p.479）、増田は、廻間III式の第一段階と位置付ける（増田1997,p.12,p.14）。の[[前方後方墳]]である。 == 概要 == 鷺山古墳は東西方向におよそ150m、南北方向に500mほどの周囲の水田面から比高差7mで「く」の字形の小高い丘の頂点に築かれており、後方部の墳丘の頂点に標高84.42mの[[三角点]]がある。 鷺山古墳の全長は約60m、後方部の墳丘の高さは5.4m、後方部の一辺が37m、くびれ部の幅11mで前方部につながる。後方部は、厳密に方形ではなく、隅の部分が切り落とされたプランを呈している。前方部は初期古墳独特の墳丘が低いばち型（扇状にひらく形）で、10.5mまで後方部に対して74度でひらき、35度に屈曲しながら13mほど外側にひらく。前方部前面の最大幅は、30mである。前方部の盛土は後方部に近い部分が高くされ、標高80.3mであった。前方部全面の扇形に広がる部分は一段低くなっている。周溝がほぼ全周すると考えられ、幅は4.3m、深さ1mほどである。 前方部と後方部をつなぐくびれ部の西側の周溝から焼成前[[底部穿孔土器|底部穿孔]]の二重口縁壺が出土した。[[口縁部]]直径25cm、口縁部下端18cm、胴部の最大径が28.5cm、器高34cmで焼成後に赤彩されている。口縁部の「縁帯」の幅は8cmをはかり、直径1.8cmほどの円形の透かし孔が6対計12個あけられている。底部は6.4cmで焼成前に予めあけられた孔となっている。 そのほか、完形品の碗型土器、二重口縁壺の口縁部破片や、東海地方独特の[[S字状口縁台付甕]]の胴部と脚部をつなぐ部分の出土など初期古墳の特色を示す遺物が出土している。二重口縁の壺型土器と碗型土器は葬送儀礼の際に用いられ、墳丘に置かれたものが転落して周溝から発見されたと考えられる。 File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|鷺山古墳の標柱 File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|鷺山古墳遠景 File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|鷺山古墳後方部墳頂部にある三角点 == 脚注 ==
== 参考文献 == {{参照方法|date=2023年4月|section=1}} *「鷺山古墳」『埼玉県古式古墳調査報告書』所収、埼玉県史編纂室、1986年（昭和61年） *小沢洋「南関東地方の前方後方墳」『前方後方墳を考える』第一分冊、海考古学フォーラム、1995年（平成7年） *増田逸郎「古墳出現期の北武蔵-前方後方墳成立の要因-」『調査研究報告第10号』[[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|埼玉県立さきたま資料館]]、1997年（平成9年） == 関連項目 == * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さきやまこふん ほんしようし}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = Location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * **"Sagiyama Kofun."** *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]]

File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = Location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = Location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]]

Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period burial mound. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation point at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Burial Mounds in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]
