{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, aqqroximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, aqqroximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, a11roximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, a11roximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sagi-Yama Kofun (Honjo)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|scale:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | construction_period = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-typed hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-typed with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-typed pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-typed jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-typed pottery * S-typed jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Numerous early Kofun-period artifacts, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. Artifacts from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == Artifacts == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==

Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following artifacts were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | burial goods = Numerous early Kofun-period burial goods, including double-rimmed jars | designation = None | map = Japan Saitama | label = Sagiyama Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | label_position = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Honjo|ja|本庄市}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}} to early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, [[Category:pp error]]approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns|ja|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
