Numerous early Kofun-period burial goods, including double-rimmed jars | Historic Site = None | map = Japan Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Sagiyama Kofun (Honjo)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[sn:agiyama Kofun (Honjo)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}}

The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Sagiyama Kofun (Honjo)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[sn:agiyama Kofun (Honjo)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Numerous early Kofun-period burial goods, including double-rimmed jars | Historic Site = None | map = Japan Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Sagiyama Kofun (Honjo)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[sn:agiyama Kofun (Honjo)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}}

Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11676426}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Numerous early Kofun-period burial goods, including double-rimmed jars | Historic Site = None | map = Japan Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11676426}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sagiyama Kofun (鷺山古墳) | image = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun 1.jpg|250px]] | caption = Close-up view of the rear section of Sagiyama Kofun | location = Kodama-machi, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Honjo City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|36|12|30.4|N|139|9|43.7|E|map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Numerous early Kofun-period burial goods, including double-rimmed jars | Historic Site = None | map = Japan Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11676426}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late 3rd to early 4th century | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Numerous early Kofun-period burial goods, including double-rimmed jars | Historic Site = None | map = Japan Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Honjō, Saitama|Honjo]], Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]], it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]], 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11676426}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need Saitama | map_dot_label = Sagiyama Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南西.svg | map_dot_label = bottom | map_caption = location in Saitama Prefecture }} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Honjō, Saitama|en|Honjō, Saitama|WD=Q873565|lt=Honjo}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. File:The 'Obelisk'signpole of Sagiyama Kofun tumulus.jpg|Signpost at Sagiyama Kofun File:Sagiyama Kofun2.jpg|Distant view of Sagiyama Kofun File:Trig point of the top of Sagiyama Kofun.jpg|Triangulation station at the top of Sagiyama Kofun's rear section == References ==
== Bibliography == * '''"Sagiyama Kofun."''' *Report on Ancient Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture,* Saitama Prefectural Historical Compilation Office, 1986. * Hiroshi Ozawa, "Zenpō-kōhō-fun in Southern Kanto," *Considering Zenpō-kōhō-fun,* Vol. 1, Marine Archaeology Forum, 1995. * Itsuro Masuda, "The Emergence of Kofun in Northern Musashi: Factors in the Formation of Zenpō-kōhō-fun," *Research Report No. 10,* {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds|WD=Q5017775|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns}}, 1997. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鷺山古墳 (本庄市)|version=96035940|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鷺山古墳 (本庄市)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Sagiyama kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11676426}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Saitama|WD=Q873565|lt=Honjo|1=Honjō, Saitama}}, Kodama Town, Shimo-Asami, Sagiyama, Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Constructed during the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, it is an early Kofun-period Kofun. == Overview == Sagiyama Kofun is situated at the peak of a small "ku"-shaped hill, approximately 150 meters from east to west and 500 meters from north to south, rising 7 meters above the surrounding rice fields. The highest point of the rear section reaches 84.42 meters above sea level, marked by a triangulation point. The total length of the kofun is about 60 meters. The rear section of the mound stands 5.4 meters high with a base width of 37 meters. The constricted middle section connects to the front section, which is fan-shaped with a low-profile characteristic of early kofun. The front section extends 10.5 meters at a 74-degree angle from the rear section before bending outward at 35 degrees over 13 meters. The maximum width of the front section is 30 meters, with higher embankments near the rear section at an elevation of 80.3 meters. A moat, approximately 4.3 meters wide and 1 meter deep, is believed to encircle the mound. burial goods from the mound include a double-rimmed jar with a pierced bottom recovered from the western moat of the constricted section. This jar has a rim diameter of 25 cm, a lower rim diameter of 18 cm, and a body diameter of 28.5 cm. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region. Standing 34 cm tall, the jar features 12 circular pierced holes in pairs along an 8 cm-wide rim band and a pierced base of 6.4 cm diameter. Other findings include intact bowl-shaped pottery and fragments of double-rimmed jars, as well as S-shaped jars typical of the Tokai region. These items, likely used in burial rituals, are thought to have fallen into the moat after being placed on the mound. == burial goods == The following burial goods were recovered from the site: * Double-rimmed jar with pierced base * Bowl-shaped pottery * S-shaped jar fragments * Additional pottery fragments characteristic of early Kofun-period burial customs These items indicate connections between Sagiyama Kofun and other early burial sites in the southern Kanto region.
